title: Map relative tyre labels to absolute Pirelli C-compounds
summary: The dataset uses HARD/MEDIUM/SOFT, but those labels are event-relative; Pirelli nominates different C1-C5/C6 compounds per race, and 2023-2025 tyre ranges changed.
rationale: The dataset uses HARD/MEDIUM/SOFT, but those labels are event-relative; Pirelli nominates different C1-C5/C6 compounds per race, and 2023-2025 tyre ranges changed. A MEDIUM at one GP can be a different physical tyre than a MEDIUM elsewhere, so fixed expected-life maps blur degradation signals.
implementation_hint: Add a Race-Year lookup from public Pirelli/F1 compound announcements mapping HARD/MEDIUM/SOFT to absolute C-code, hardness rank, adjacent/non-adjacent allocation flag, and season tyre-era indicators. Replace or augment current SOFT/MEDIUM/HARD life constants with C-code-specific and race-specific priors.
expected_effect: Better cross-race generalization and cleaner interactions between TyreLife, degradation, Race, and Year, especially for 2025 races with unusual allocations.
risk: Manual lookup errors or missing race-name normalization can hurt; keep unknowns as explicit categories and test with Race-Year grouped CV.
